subject: WarpWorker crash with *.tiff files (Warp 1.0.7)
body:
Hi Everyone,

We recently got Warp 1.0.7 running (after Dimitry provided a new 
libtensorflow.dll and it seems to run okay in our mini test with *.mrc 
files; however, a dataset with *.tiff files seems to be causing issues.  We 
get an almost immediate Windows crash error for WarpWorker (after the 
BoxNet model loads).  The WarpWorker window shows errors that seem to 
relate to TIFFs (see attached file).

When I look into the Windows event manager, there seem to be two associated 
errors:

Windows cannot access the file  for one of the following reasons: there is 
a problem with the network connection, the disk that the file is stored on, 
or the storage drivers installed on this computer; or the disk is missing. 
Windows closed the program WarpWorker because of this error.


Program: WarpWorker

File:


The error value is listed in the Additional Data section.

User Action

1. Open the file again. This situation might be a temporary problem that 
corrects itself when the program runs again.

2. If the file still cannot be accessed and

        - It is on the network, your network administrator should verify 
that there is not a problem with the network and that the server can be 
contacted.

        - It is on a removable disk, for example, a floppy disk or CD-ROM, 
verify that the disk is fully inserted into the computer.

3. Check and repair the file system by running CHKDSK. To run CHKDSK, click 
Start, click Run, type CMD, and then click OK. At the command prompt, type 
CHKDSK /F, and then press ENTER.

4. If the problem persists, restore the file from a backup copy.

5. Determine whether other files on the same disk can be opened. If not, 
the disk might be damaged. If it is a hard disk, contact your administrator 
or computer hardware vendor for further assistance.


Additional Data

Error value: 00000000

Disk type: 0

and

Faulting application name: WarpWorker.exe, version: 1.0.0.0, time stamp: 
0xad558e28

Faulting module name: tiff.dll, version: 0.0.0.0, time stamp: 0x5ccc6b21

Exception code: 0xc000001d

Fault offset: 0x0000000000026b3c

Faulting process ID: 0x15e4

Faulting application start time: 0x01d602ae6f9865ab

Faulting application path: C:\warp_107\WarpWorker.exe

Faulting module path: C:\warp_107\tiff.dll

Report ID: d74e39b0-b83c-46c8-8629-31b4bd9ff5bf

Faulting package full name:

Faulting package-relative application ID:



These errors occur when the data is being accessed over the network and 
also when the data is copied locally to the Warp computer (2 x Intel Xeon 
E5-2667 v2, 3.30 GHz each
3 x GeForce GTX 1080 Ti with driver version 442.59, 128 GB RAM, Windows 10 
Pro N).  Has anyone seen anything like this and know what could be causing 
it?

Thank you!

Cheers,
Kendra Leigh

Received "SetHeaderlessParams", with 3 arguments, for GPU #0, 9322 MB free
